seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in OpenAI Assistants API Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in OpenAI Assistants API agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Assistants API, RAG Retrieval Quality issues manifest when the built-in file search tool retrieves irrelevant, outdated, or incomplete documents, leading agents to generate inaccurate responses. This is exacerbated by the API's opaque retrieval logic and lack of built-in relevance scoring, causing agents to confidently cite wrong files. Thread context bloat from poor retrievals compounds the problem, silently degrading performance as irrelevant chunks accumulate.

howItManifests.scenario: File search retrieves semantically similar but factually outdated documents
howItManifests.symptom: Agent cites 2022 pricing data for 2024 product queries

howItManifests.scenario: Broad queries match high-volume irrelevant files
howItManifests.symptom: 20KB thread responses dominated by boilerplate docs

howItManifests.scenario: No chunking strategy leads to oversized document retrievals
howItManifests.symptom: Single 500KB PDF consumes entire thread context

howItManifests.scenario: Missing metadata filtering retrieves internal docs for customer queries

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Score Check
detection.description: Extracts file citations from assistant responses and computes semantic similarity to query
detection.code:
import openai
import numpy as np
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI()

def check_retrieval_relevance(thread_id: str, query: str) -> dict:
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
    latest_msg = messages.data[0]
    
    if latest_msg.annotations:
        cited_files = [ann.file_citation.file_id for ann in latest_msg.annotations 
                      if ann.type == 'file_citation']
        
        # Fetch file content (simplified)
        retrieved_content = ""
        for file_id in cited_files[:3]:  # Limit for perf
            file = client.files.content(file_id)
            retrieved_content += file.text[:2000]
        
        # Compute similarity
        emb_query = client.embeddings.create(input=query, model='text-embedding-3-small').data[0].embedding
        emb_retrieved = client.embeddings.create(input=retrieved_content, model='text-embedding-3-small').data[0].embedding
        
        similarity = np.dot(emb_query, emb_retrieved) / (np.linalg.norm(emb_query) * np.linalg.norm(emb_retrieved))
        
        return {'relevance_score': similarity, 'threshold_met': similarity > 0.75}
    return {'relevance_score': 0, 'threshold_met': False}

detection.name: File Citation Coverage Analysis
detection.description: Verifies if response cites relevant files and measures citation density
detection.code:
import re
from typing import List, Dict

def analyze_citation_coverage(response_text: str, expected_files: List[str]) -> Dict:
    citations = re.findall(r'\[\[[Citation\s*(\d+)\]\]\]', response_text)
    citation_count = len(set(citations))
    
    total_length = len(response_text)
    factual_sentences = len([s for s in response_text.split('.') if len(s) > 20])
    
    coverage_ratio = citation_count / max(factual_sentences, 1)
    
    return {
        'citation_count': citation_count,
        'coverage_ratio': coverage_ratio,
        'healthy': coverage_ratio > 0.3 and citation_count > 0
    }

fixes.name: Post-Retrieval Reranking
fixes.description: Re-ranks retrieved files by semantic relevance before response generation
fixes.code:
import operator

def rerank_retrievals(query: str, file_results: list) -> list:
    client = OpenAI()
    
    reranked = []
    for file_info in file_results:
        # Get file preview
        file_content = client.files.content(file_info.id).text[:1000]
        
        emb_query = client.embeddings.create(input=query, model='text-embedding-3-small')
        emb_file = client.embeddings.create(input=file_content, model='text-embedding-3-small')
        
        score = np.dot(emb_query.data[0].embedding, emb_file.data[0].embedding)
        reranked.append((file_info, score))
    
    return [f for f, s in sorted(reranked, key=operator.itemgetter(1), reverse=True)[:2]]

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a webhook on OpenAI Assistants run completion events, automatically analyzing every agent response for retrieval quality issues. It extracts file citations, computes semantic relevance scores against the original query, and flags responses with low coverage (<30%) or poor similarity (<0.75 cosine). Beyond detection, Vex can trigger automated remediation flows like query rewriting or fallback to curated Q&A datasets.

The platform maintains historical baselines per assistant, alerting when retrieval quality drops below organizational thresholds. Integration takes <5 minutes with the vex-sdk, and provides production-ready dashboards for RAG health monitoring across all deployed assistants.
